0.q: Under 300 mM NaCl, the SOD activity of all lines increased due to salt stress, but the SOD activity increased significantly in transgenic lines C7 and C57, compared with that in WT plants.
our results demonstrated that GsCBRLK and GsMSRB5a interacted with each other, and activated ROS signaling under carbonate alkaline stress.

4.q: Further investigations elucidated that GsMSRB5a and GsCBRLK inhibited reactive oxygen species (ROS) accumulation by modifying the expression of ROS signaling, biosynthesis and scavenging genes.

5.q: overexpressing the GsEXPB1 gene significantly enhanced the antioxidant enzyme activities and the accumulation of antioxidant substances in soybean hairy roots, while the roots silencing the GmEXPB4 gene by RNAi showed the opposite result.

6.q: CAT and APX were activated with a significant higher level in the Gs5PTase8-transgenic Arabidopsis plants than the wild type under salt treatment, suggesting that Gs5PTase8 would regulate ROS signaling probably in the apoplast.

7.q: It was also found that GsTIFY10 could repress JA signal transduction. The roots of plants overexpressing GsTIFY10 grew longer than wild-type in the presence of MeJA, and some JA response and JA biosynthesis marker genes were suppressed in the GsTIFY10 overexpression lines.

9.q: Binding assays demonstrated that calmodulin binds to GsCBRLK with an affinity of 25.9 nM in a calcium-dependent manner and the binding motif lies between amino acids 147 to 169 within subdomain II of the kinase domain.

10.q: fluorescence was found on the PM of GsCBRLK–YFPN/GsMSRB5a–YFPC co-transformed protoplasts. These results demonstrated a physical interaction between GsCBRLK and GsMSRB5a on the PM of plant cells.

11.q: The growth assays indicated that GsMSRB5a.1, GsMSRB5a.2 and GsMSRB5b were able to interact with GsCBRLK, but GsMSRB1, GsMSRB2a, GsMSRB2b.1 and GsMSRB2b.2 could not.

12.q: the Y2H assays revealed that the yeast cells co-expressing NubG-GsBET11a and GsCRCK1a/1b/1c/1d-Cub could grow... indicating that GsBET11a interacted with all these four GsCRCK1 proteins.

15.q: we identified GsSRF2, which encodes a RING-domain E3 ligase that interacts with GsSnRK1. BiFC, pull-down, and co-IP assays this interaction in the cytoplasm and at the plasma membrane.

16.q: A transcription factor protein GsNAC83 was identified to interact with both GsMYST1 and GsSnRK1.

19.q: Inositol polyphosphate 5-phosphatases (5PTases) are one of the key components in inositol pathway. A novel Gs5PTase8 was cloned from Glycine soja and characterized for its function in salt responses.

21.q: the expression levels of GsHKT1-1 and GsSOS1 were significantly upregulated in GsWRKY23-OE plants... and GsCLC-c2 and GsCLC-b2 exhibited the opposite trend

22.q: the expression levels of GsHKT1-1 and GsSOS1 were significantly upregulated in GsWRKY23-OE plants... and GsCLC-c2 and GsCLC-b2 exhibited the opposite trend

23.q: Biochemical analyses revealed that GsSnRK1 phosphorylates GsSRF2 at Thr514.

24.q: GsMYST1 was able to be phosphorylated at the Ser44 site by GsSnRK1 and demonstrated in vivo acetyltransferase activity in transgenic soybean roots revealed by an anti-H4ace antibody.

25.q: calcium/calmodulin positively regulates GsCBRLK kinase activity through direct interaction between the calmodulin-binding domain and calmodulin.

26.q: the activated GsSnRK1 subsequently phosphorylated GsMYST1 to enhance its acetyltransferase activity which may epigenetically promote the target gene expression.

27.q: the expression levels of GsHKT1-1 and GsSOS1 were significantly upregulated in GsWRKY23-OE plants in contrast to EV plants but were clearly downregulated in GsWRKY23-RNAi plants

28.q: the expression levels of GsHKT1-1 and GsSOS1 were significantly upregulated in GsWRKY23-OE plants in contrast to EV plants but were clearly downregulated in GsWRKY23-RNAi plants

29.q: these results highlight the role of GsCLC-c2 in regulating anionic homeostasis in NaCl-stressed transgenic Arabidopsis and soybean composite plants to maintain lower Cl−/NO3− ratios in shoots, thus conferring enhanced Cl−/salt tolerance.

GsCLC-c2 over-expression contributed to Cl− and NO3− homeostasis, and therefore conferred salt tolerance, through increasing the accumulation of Cl− in the roots, thereby reducing their transportation to the shoots.

30.q: these results highlight the role of GsCLC-c2 in regulating anionic homeostasis in NaCl-stressed transgenic Arabidopsis and soybean composite plants to maintain lower Cl−/NO3− ratios in shoots
GsCLC-c2 over-expression contributed to Cl- and NO3- homeostasis, and therefore conferred salt tolerance, through increasing the accumulation of Cl- in the roots

31.q: overexpressing them reduced transgenic Arabidopsis seed germination sensitivity to salt stress. Moreover, most of these genes could improve salt tolerance of the transgenic Arabidopsis plants, corroborated by a detection of expression variation of several key genes in the salt-tolerance pathways.

32.q: overexpressing the GsEXPB1 gene significantly enhanced the antioxidant enzyme activities and the accumulation of antioxidant substances in soybean hairy roots, while the roots silencing the GmEXPB4 gene by RNAi showed the opposite result.

33.q: GsCHX19.3 functions as a K+ uptake transporter at alkali pH, and also participated in carbonate stress responses in yeast cells.

34.q: Overexpressing Gs5PTase8 reduced IP3 contents, probably due to its 5‐phosphatase activity.

35.q: GsRLCK overexpression decreased ABA sensitivity and altered expression levels of ABA-responsive genes.

36.q: The ectopic expression of Gs5PTase8 could impart ABA insensitivity on seed germination, suggesting it might be involved in ABA signaling.

37.q: Overexpressing Gs5PTase8 reduced IP3 contents, probably due to its 5‐phosphatase activity.

38.q: Co-transformation with GsCBRLK-YFPN/GsBET11a-YFPC resulted in yellow fluorescence mainly on the plasma membrane, suggesting a protein interaction between GsCBRLK and GsBET11a.

39.q: Direct interaction between GsCBRLK and GsBET11a was initially identiﬁed via yeast two-hybrid and bimolecular ﬂuorescence complementation assays.

40.q: we isolated a GsSnRK1 kinase interacting protein (GsMSTY1) which is phylogenetically homologous with histone acetyltransferase MYST family

41.q: Sequence identities of DA1-like paralogs in a soybean showed a large difference ranging from 44.1 to 97.7 %.

42.q: the degradation of IP3 or PI(4,5)P2 by Gs5PTase8 may maintain the ion homoeostasis of plants under salt stress

43.q: Phenotypic assays demonstrated that GsRLCK overexpression decreased ABA sensitivity and altered expression levels of ABA-responsive genes.

44.q: results from yeast complementation assays revealed that GsCHX19.3 functioned as a K+ uptake transporter at alkali pH

45.q: These enzymes catalyze the conjugation of glutathione (GSH) to the electrophilic groups of a large variety of hydrophobic molecules to detoxify cells.

46.q: phosphorylation of GsMYST1 is required for it to acetylate histone H4 on the target genes to upregulate expression of the stress-related genes

47.q: the GmCHX1 transgenic lines also maintain a lower Na+/K+ ratio under salt stress, supporting our hypothesis that GmCHX1 is involved in ion homeostasis.

48.q: GnSnRK1-mediated phosphorylation enhanced GsSRF2 ubiquitination and promoted its proteasomal degradation.
